title: Plant haul from the farmers market
body: Plant haul!! Since we got the shelves up I've been itching to go to Rocky Mountain Fresh's booth at the Boulder Farmers Market. They have a great collection of succulents and cactuses and I added a bunch to the collection today 😁.
These basically all fit on the same two empty shelves I had left over from yesterday, so there's still plenty of room to grow the collection. Very excited!

A few standouts: a spineless sand-dollar Astrophytum, a fan-crested hybrid cactus tagged 'TPM × Althea', a pair of living stones in different markings, my first fuzzy bear's paw, and a red African milk tree. Every plant below has its own dex entry now — tap through to the care sheets.

shots.cap: The whole haul, fresh off the market table.
Another Zebra Haworthia to join the dish garden.
Ladyfinger Cactus — the uncrested cousin of my brain cactus.
Living Stones (Lithops) — two patterns in one pot.
Cobweb Houseleek, laced in white webbing.
A little Prickly Pear budding new pads.
Bear's Paw — fuzzy leaves with reddish claw tips.
African Milk Tree, the red 'Rubra' form.
Balloon Cactus — blue-green ball with a woolly crown.
